title: glmOJ: Count Regression Modeling
text: glmOJ provides streamlined workflow fitting, diagnosing, interpreting count regression models. six supported families : general-purpose wrapper countGLM() fits six selects best user-chosen criterion (decide): "BIC" (default), "AIC", "LogLik", "McFadden" (McFadden pseudo-R²). Zero-inflated Tweedie always fitted alongside base Tweedie model (rather gated DHARMa test) Tweedie’s dispersion parameter can absorb excess zeros, making DHARMa zero-inflation test unreliable family.

title: glmOJ: Count Regression Modeling
text: four model families share assumption predictors strongly collinear. countGLM() automatically computes Variance Inflation Factors stores $vif. avoid false positives structural collinearity — expected whenever interaction polynomial terms present — VIF computed OLS model retains order-1 (main-effect) terms formula. Offset terms excluded well. VIF = 1 means predictor uncorrelated others; values ~5 generally acceptable. VIF > 5 triggers warning. predictors well threshold, confirming multicollinearity concern model. illustrate interaction-term handling, fitting Poisson model quadratic term inflate VIF underlying predictors:
code: result_cam$vif #>                pnhblk                pnhwht      total_crime_rate  #>              1.696259              2.292616              1.152382  #>                  hinc                  pvac  modal_zoneindustrial  #>              1.592488              1.093179              1.115794  #>       modal_zonemixed      modal_zonepublic modal_zoneresidential  #>              1.101121              1.151036              1.495288  #>       modal_zoneroads  #>              1.145420 result_quad <- suppressWarnings(countGLM(   cam_count ~ pnhblk +     pnhwht +     total_crime_rate +     I(total_crime_rate^2) +     offset(log_road_length),   data = Dahir25.dat )) result_quad$vif # only main-effect terms: pnhblk, pnhwht, total_crime_rate #>                pnhblk                pnhwht      total_crime_rate  #>              1.683595              1.637775              1.726555  #> I(total_crime_rate^2)  #>              1.648745

title: glmOJ: Count Regression Modeling
text: countGLM() fits six families. Unlike Poisson negative binomial, zeroinfl_tweedie always fitted alongside tweedie: glmmTMB can absorb excess zeros inflating dispersion parameter ϕ\phi, DHARMa zero-inflation test unreliable Tweedie. AIC/BIC arbitrates base zero-inflated Tweedie fits directly. recommendation confirms zeroinfl_tweedie selected notes always included candidate set regardless DHARMa result.

title: glmOJ: Count Regression Modeling
text: single coefficient enough describe interaction: effect one predictor depends value another. untangle_interaction() produces right post-hoc summary case — categorical × categorical, categorical × continuous, continuous × continuous — one call. accepts model fit returned package (raw glm / glmmTMB object) pair interacting variables. default function average categorical predictor outside interaction interest; level reported separately. Continuous controls held mean (emmeans default). Pass names average.collapse specific categorical predictors. return Greenberg environmental-crime data fit three variants Poisson model, different interaction, illustrate three cases.

title: glmOJ — Count Regression Modeling
text: glmOJ R package provides structured workflow count semi-continuous regression modeling. wraps glm, MASS::glm.nb, pscl::zeroinfl, glmmTMB::glmmTMB fit, diagnose, interpret count regression models, output reported response scale rather link scale.

title: glmOJ — Count Regression Modeling
text: Count data — non-negative integers like number events, species, visits — require specialized regression models. glmOJ guides analyst four steps: Summarize response variable relationship predictors modeling. Fit one count regression models supported families. Assess model conditions using randomized quantile residuals (RQR) Pearson dispersion ratio. Interpret output response scale (exponentiated coefficients confidence intervals).

title: glmOJ — Count Regression Modeling
text: general-purpose countGLM() fits six families selects best user-chosen criterion (decide = "BIC" default; also accepts "AIC", "LogLik", "McFadden"), annotated dispersion zero-inflation diagnostics.

title: glmOJ — Count Regression Modeling
text: individual fitter returns exponentiated coefficients 95% Wald confidence intervals, randomized quantile residuals, Pearson dispersion ratio, two-panel diagnostic plot (fitted values vs. RQR | normal Q-Q RQR). poissonGLM(formula, data) — Poisson regression via stats::glm. negbinGLM(formula, data) — Negative binomial regression via MASS::glm.nb. zeroinflPoissonGLM(formula, data, ziformula) — Zero-inflated Poisson via pscl::zeroinfl. Separate coefficient tables count zero-inflation components. zeroinflNegbinGLM(formula, data, ziformula) — Zero-inflated negative binomial via pscl::zeroinfl. tweedieGLM(formula, data) — Tweedie GLM via glmmTMB::glmmTMB. Returns estimated dispersion phi power parameter p. zeroinflTweedieGLM(formula, data, ziformula) — Zero-inflated Tweedie via glmmTMB::glmmTMB. Separate coefficient tables count zero-inflation components.

previous_headings: Package functions
title: glmOJ — Count Regression Modeling
text: countGLM(formula, data, ziformula, decide = "BIC") — Fits six families, selects best criterion decide ("BIC", "AIC", "LogLik", "McFadden"), returns plain-language recommendation informed Pearson dispersion ratio observed vs. expected zero counts. Zero-inflated Tweedie always fitted alongside base Tweedie; ZI Poisson ZI Negative Binomial fitted DHARMa detects significant zero-inflation base counterpart.

title: glmOJ — Count Regression Modeling
text: model fitters compute: Randomized quantile residuals (RQR) — well-fitting model, RQRs approximately standard normal. diagnostic plot shows fitted values (checking structure) normal Q-Q plot. Pearson dispersion ratio — Pearson chi-squared divided residual degrees freedom. Values near 1 consistent assumed model; values substantially 1 (rule thumb: > 1.2 Poisson fit) suggest overdispersion motivate switching negative binomial /zero-inflated model. Sample size conditions — package checks dataset enough observations relative number predictors; warning issued effective sample--parameter ratio low support reliable estimation. Variance Inflation Factors (VIFs) — VIFs computed predictor flag multicollinearity. Predictors VIF 5 flagged warning users can investigate correlated predictors interpreting coefficients.

Zero-inflated models — pass component = "count" (default) count sub-model component = "zero" zero-inflation sub-model. zero component phrase reads “odds structural zero”. countGLM objects — automatically delegates best-fitting model prints message indicating one used. Non-significant note — coefficient’s p-value exceeds 0.05, note appended: “coefficient discernibly different zero (p = …)”. term name passed predictor must match exactly string coefficient table (e.g. "EPAregion2", "EPAregion"). term found, function stops list available terms.

title: Fit and compare count regression models — countGLM
code: countGLM(formula, data, ziformula = NULL, decide = "BIC", ...)

title: Fit and compare count regression models — countGLM
text: formula model formula count component (e.g. y ~ x1 + x2). response must non-negative. data data frame containing variables formula (ziformula provided). ziformula one-sided formula zero-inflation component passed zeroinflPoissonGLM(), zeroinflNegbinGLM(), zeroinflTweedieGLM() needed. NULL (default), right-hand side formula used. decide Character string specifying model-selection criterion. One "BIC" (default), "AIC", "LogLik" (log-likelihood, higher better), "McFadden" (McFadden pseudo-R², higher better). Matching case-insensitive. ... Additional arguments passed individual model fitter.

title: Fit and compare count regression models — countGLM
text: object class "countGLM", list : call matched call. fits named list successfully fitted model objects. Base models (poisson, negbin, tweedie) always attempted. zeroinfl_poisson zeroinfl_negbin fitted DHARMa zero-inflation test flags base model (p < 0.05). zeroinfl_tweedie always fitted alongside tweedie glmmTMB's flexible dispersion makes DHARMa test unreliable family. model failed converge omitted. Base model fits include diagnostics$zi_test populated DHARMa test. aic_table named numeric vector AICs, sorted ascending. bic_table named numeric vector BICs, sorted ascending. metric_table named numeric vector selection metric values (criterion named decide), sorted best-first. decide normalised (lower-case) name selection criterion actually used. best_model Character name model selected decide. recommendation plain-language character string explaining selection, including criterion value, dispersion context, zero-inflation test results. vif Named numeric vector Variance Inflation Factors main-effect predictors formula (interaction polynomial terms excluded). NULL fewer two main-effect predictors present. warning issued VIF > 5.

title: Fit and compare count regression models — countGLM
text: Workflow: countGLM() fits Poisson, negative binomial, Tweedie base models. runs DHARMa simulation test zero-inflation successful base model. Poisson negative binomial, zero-inflated counterpart fitted zero-inflation detected (p < 0.05). Tweedie, zero-inflated counterpart always fitted alongside base model: glmmTMB can absorb excess zeros inflating dispersion parameter phi, makes DHARMa ZI test unreliable family. surviving models compared decide. Model selection: model best value decide chosen. "AIC" "BIC" model lowest value wins; "LogLik" "McFadden" model highest value wins. AIC BIC always computed displayed regardless decide. decide = "McFadden", intercept-null models fitted family compute pseudo-R². Individual models support print(), summary(), plot().

title: Fit and compare count regression models — countGLM
code: df <- data.frame(   y  = c(0L, 1L, 2L, 3L, 5L, 0L, 2L, 4L, 1L, 3L),   x1 = c(1.2, -0.4, 0.8, -1.1, 2.0, 0.3, -0.9, 1.5, -0.2, 0.7) ) result <- suppressWarnings(countGLM(y ~ x1, data = df))      # default: BIC result <- suppressWarnings(countGLM(y ~ x1, data = df, decide = "AIC")) result <- suppressWarnings(countGLM(y ~ x1, data = df, decide = "McFadden")) print(result) #>  #> Call: #> countGLM(formula = y ~ x1, data = df, decide = "McFadden") #>  #> Model comparison (sorted by McFadden R2 (descending)): #>    model   AIC   BIC McFadden R2 #>  poisson 39.02 39.63      0.0459 #>   negbin 41.02 41.93      0.0407 #>  #> Selected model: poisson #>  #> Recommendation: #>   Poisson was selected by McFadden R² (McFadden R² = 0.0459). The #>   Poisson dispersion ratio is 1.17, consistent with equidispersion. No #>   significant zero-inflation detected for Poisson or Negative Binomial. #>  summary(result) #> Summary of selected model (poisson): #>  #>  #> Call: #> poissonGLM(formula = formula, data = data, assessZeroInflation = FALSE) #>  #> Model family: poissonGLM  #>  #> Coefficients (on response scale): #>         term exp.coef lower.95 upper.95 p.value stars #>  (Intercept)   1.7989   1.0683   3.0290  0.0272     * #>           x1   1.3396   0.8572   2.0936  0.1993       #>  #> Dispersion ratio: 1.1658 #> AIC: 39.02

title: Fit a negative binomial regression model — negbinGLM
text: object class c("negbinGLM", "countGLMfit"), list : call matched call. model underlying MASS::glm.nb fit object. summary result summary() fitted model. theta estimated negative binomial dispersion parameter (smaller values indicate overdispersion). coefficients data frame columns term, exp.coef, lower.95, upper.95 (response/exponentiated scale). diagnostics list : rqr Numeric vector randomized quantile residuals. dispersion_ratio Pearson chi-squared / df.residual. plot Patchwork ggplot: fitted vs RQR histo-QQ. r2_plot Squared Pearson residuals vs fitted values. zi_test assessZeroInflation = TRUE, list detected (logical), p_value (numeric), plot (ggplot histogram DHARMa simulated zero proportions vs observed). NULL assessZeroInflation = FALSE. aic AIC fitted model. bic BIC fitted model.

title: Fit a negative binomial regression model — negbinGLM
text: Coefficient interpretation: Negative binomial regression models log expected count. Exponentiating coefficient gives multiplicative change expected count one-unit increase predictor. use: Negative binomial appropriate count data show overdispersion (variance > mean). Pearson dispersion ratio poissonGLM() substantially 1 (rule thumb: > 1.5) common signal. negative binomial adds free parameter theta model extra variance. count data complex variance structures, consider tweedieGLM(). zero-inflation also detected, consider zeroinflNegbinGLM().

what: Fit a Tweedie regression model — tweedieGLM
title: Fit a Tweedie regression model — tweedieGLM
text: Fits Tweedie GLM (via glmmTMB::glmmTMB()) returns model coefficients response scale (exponentiated), randomized quantile residuals (RQR), estimated dispersion (phi) power (p) parameters, diagnostic plots. Tweedie family generalises Poisson Gamma distributions well-suited non-negative count data complex variance structures.

title: Fit a Tweedie regression model — tweedieGLM
code: tweedieGLM(formula, data, assessZeroInflation = TRUE, ...)

title: Fit a Tweedie regression model — tweedieGLM
text: formula model formula (e.g. y ~ x1 + x2). response must non-negative. data data frame containing variables formula. assessZeroInflation Logical; TRUE (default), runs DHARMa simulation-based zero-inflation test fitting. Issues warning significant zero-inflation detected adds zi_test returned diagnostics. Set FALSE calling countGLM(), performs zero-inflation assessment. ... Additional arguments passed glmmTMB::glmmTMB().

title: Fit a Tweedie regression model — tweedieGLM
text: object class c("tweedieGLM", "countGLMfit"), list : call matched call. model underlying glmmTMB::glmmTMB fit object. summary result summary() fitted model. phi estimated Tweedie dispersion parameter (phi). p estimated Tweedie power parameter (p), restricted (1, 2). Values near 1 resemble Poisson; values near 2 resemble Gamma. NA parameter extracted fit. coefficients data frame columns term, exp.coef, lower.95, upper.95, p.value, stars (response/exponentiated scale). diagnostics list : rqr Numeric vector randomized quantile residuals (DHARMa simulation-based, converted normal scale). dispersion_ratio Pearson chi-squared / df.residual. plot Patchwork ggplot: fitted vs RQR histo-QQ. r2_plot Squared Pearson residuals vs fitted values. zi_test assessZeroInflation = TRUE, list detected (logical), p_value (numeric), plot (ggplot histogram DHARMa simulated zero proportions vs observed). NULL assessZeroInflation = FALSE. aic AIC fitted model. bic BIC fitted model.

title: Fit a Tweedie regression model — tweedieGLM
text: Three parameters: Tweedie family characterised three estimated quantities: regression coefficients (mean structure, via log link), dispersion phi (Var(Y) = phi * mu^p), power p (1 < p < 2). p close 1, variance structure resembles Poisson; close 2, resembles Gamma. Coefficient interpretation: Exponentiating coefficient gives multiplicative change expected response one-unit increase predictor, holding predictors constant. use: Tweedie regression appropriate non-negative count data complex variance structures well captured Poisson negative binomial. zero-inflation also present, consider zeroinflTweedieGLM().

title: Fit a Tweedie regression model — tweedieGLM
code: df <- data.frame(   y  = c(0, 0, 1.5, 3.2, 5.8, 0, 0.9, 4.1, 0, 2.7),   x1 = c(1.2, -0.4, 0.8, -1.1, 2.0, 0.3, -0.9, 1.5, -0.2, 0.7) ) fit <- suppressWarnings(tweedieGLM(y ~ x1, data = df)) print(fit) #>  #> Call: #> tweedieGLM(formula = y ~ x1, data = df) #>  #> Model family: tweedieGLM #>  #> Coefficients (on response scale): #>         term exp.coef lower.95 upper.95 p.value stars #>  (Intercept)   1.2700   0.5923   2.7229  0.5391       #>           x1   1.7403   0.9555   3.1698  0.0701     . #>  #> Dispersion (phi): 1.4059 #> Power (p): 1.0420 #> Dispersion ratio: 2.0335 #> AIC: 40.64 plot(fit)

title: Fit a zero-inflated negative binomial regression model — zeroinflNegbinGLM
text: Coefficient interpretation: Count component: exponentiating coefficient gives multiplicative change expected count among non-structural-zero observations, one-unit increase predictor, adjusting simultaneous linear changes predictors. example, 1.5 means 50% higher expected count. Zero component: exponentiating coefficient gives multiplicative change odds structural zero (vs. entering count process) one-unit increase predictor. use: Zero-inflated negative binomial handles excess zeros overdispersion non-zero counts.
